Build a HookRegistry from a settings.json hooks block

Source: R/hooks.R
dot-hooks_from_settings.Rd

Parses a declarative hooks specification (as loaded from settings.json) into a live HookRegistry. The expected shape mirrors Claude Code:

"hooks": {
  "PreToolUse":  [{ "command": "echo pre  >> /tmp/hooks.log" }],
  "PostToolUse": [{ "command": "echo post >> /tmp/hooks.log", "pattern": "Bash" }]
}

Each entry's command is run via the shell when the event fires; pattern (optional) limits a tool hook to matching tool names.

Usage

.hooks_from_settings(settings)

Arguments

settings

List from load_settings() (uses settings$hooks).

Value

A HookRegistry, or NULL if no valid hooks are declared.

On this page
